title: Sars-CoV2 RNA purification with homemade SPRI beads for RT-qPCR test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbc7j","text":"The current SARS-CoV2 epidemic calls for large scale viral tests. The current testing procedure for the presence of virions involves several steps - pharyngeal/nasal swab, cell/viral lysis, RNA extraction, and an rtPCR assay targeting the viral genome and a human gene as an internal sample control. Improvement of any of these steps in terms of cost, processing time, or reagent availability, could result in a significant increase of testing capacity world-wide.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cq9k","text":"Here, we describe a rapid and efficient home made SPRI-based RNA extraction method from a lysed sample. Our approach can be fully automated, is quick (\u003c30 min for 96 samples) and cheap (\u003c1$ per sample), and was tested successfully on more than 250 clinical samples with approved rt-qPCR detection kits.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5fo5","text":"Note that different sources of viral samples arrive in different buffers. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4j20l","text":"This protocol was tested on samples derived by a swab into a standard Viral Transport Medium collection tube, and then mixed 1:1 with Zymo RNA/DNA shield for lysis. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":70,"length":22},{"key":1,"offset":129,"length":24}],"data":[]},{"key":"7r4jc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5v2od","text":"This is a robot-compatible protocol. See attached zipped folder containing the robotic script and worktable for running the protocol on an EVO / Evoware platform and a pdf describing the robot configration and a human-readable protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dot5b","text":"The protocol is designed to be adaptable to any robotic platform with a 96 channel arm and a manipulator arm capable of manipulating the plate containing the magnetic beads.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D6CBA1F07C0D11EA900615623CA1E9B0","url":"https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/downloads/Viral-Transport-Medium.pdf"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"F3647E907C0D11EA900615623CA1E9B0","url":"https://www.zymoresearch.com/collections/dna-rna-shield/products/dna-rna-shield"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"41fd5","text":"To prepare a large batch of home-made SPRI beads from the Sera-Mag™ Magnetic SpeedBeads follow our protocol - http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bes2jege","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":110,"length":48}],"data":[]},{"key":"jub7","text":"Take out SPRI beads from 4c storage for 30 minutes to arrive at room temperature","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ce6nq","text":"Prepare fresh 80% EtOH (250 µl / sample)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ddhbh","text":"Verify you have enough Lysis/binding buffer (equal to the total volume of samples)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"57D1FF5B7D9511EABD620242AC110002","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bes2jege"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8anf6","text":"A risk assessment should be made when working with potenetially infectious specimen. Be sure that samples were properly handled and deactivated by certified personnal. Consult your local bio-safety staff.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Sample dilution Start with clinical samples collceted in virtal transport media and inactivated by dilution in lysis buffer. Add equal volume lysis/binding buffer to samples, e.g. for 28 µl, add 28 µl lysis/binding buffer The maximal volume in the process is x3.6 the sample volume, so use the maximum amount of sample that still allows efficient robotic pipettation, depending on your setup

Bead Binding Add x0.8 volume SPRI beads to each diluted sample (e.g. 45 µl beads to 56 µl diluted sample) Mix by pipetting x10 times, avoid aeration during mixing to avoid foaming Incubate 00:10:00 at 25 °C , periodically mix by pippeting Magnetize until solution clears completely (~ 00:05:00 ) to make sure you do not loose beads Aspirate the supernatant slowly to avoid disturbing the magnetized beads and discard If beads can be seen in the tips after aspirating the supernatant or if beads are found in the waste consider the following reasons: Insufficient magnetization - Increase magnetization duration. The volume and buffer type affect the required duration. Bead scraping by the tip - Having the tip touch or scrape the beads during movement due to misalignment. A thinner tip can increase tolerance and help avoid this issue. Tip blockage and pressure buildup - If some of the tips press against the bottom of the well it will cause blockage and a buildup of negative pressure inside the tip. Once the tip moves up the pressure will cause a vortex inside the well which can disturb the beads. Have the tip at least 0.2mm above the bottom of the well and aspirate slowly

80% EtOH Wash Add 120 µL of 80% EtOH Move the plate from one side of the magnet to the other to immerse the beads in the wash solution Wait for 00:00:30 Remove the wash solution while the plate is on the magnet without disturbing the beads Repeat 80% EtOH wash (steps 3.1 - 3.4) Perform an extra aspiration of the residual EtOH with a fine tip Remove the plate from the magnet and air dry the beads to remove traces of EtOH (~ 00:02:30 minutes at 30 °C , until beads are dry by eye inspection). Avoid overdrying the beads.

Elute Add 10mM Tris pH 8 (for 56ul beads 20 µL ) at 10 °C - 22 °C Resuspend the beads fully in the elution buffer by pipetting Incubate for 00:02:00 to allow full RNA elution Place the plate on the magnet and wait for the solution to clear (~ 00:01:00 ) Keep on magnet, and transfer the supernatant to a new plate without disturbing the beads To resuspend the beads in the robotic system, we alternate between several steps: Wash the beads from the sides of the well - aspirate the elution liquid from the bottom of the well and dispense on the sides of the well above the z-line of the beads Shaking the plate Mixing by up-down pipetting about 0.5mm above the bottom of the well
creator: Alon Chappleboim
